event: Interior of a mosque in Cairo
symbols: geometric floor patterns — the Islamic artistic expression of divine order and infinity
columns receding into shadow — the depth and mystery of sacred space
description: An interior view of a mosque in Cairo, looking down a nave flanked by thick dark marble or stone columns. The floor is the most prominent element — an elaborate geometric mosaic of interlocking circles, diamonds, and other patterns in warm brown, cream, and dark tones. The columns rise to support arches that recede into shadow. A single arched window at the far end admits pale light. The walls are warm ochre and brown. The space appears empty and quiet, the emphasis on architectural geometry and the play of light on the ornate floor.
